event_name: actions-cool GitHub Actions Tag Hijack Credential Theft
confidence_reason: Migrated from older Astro-era campaign coverage and primary StepSecurity research into the Next.js/Postgres hp-posts-info contract; selected indicators are source-backed but this pass did not perform fresh artifact diffing.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: workflow resolves a mutable GitHub Action tag moved to an imposter commit

ci_cd_hunts: unexpected Bun runtime download in CI

open_questions: Public cleanup status and downstream victim count need direct confirmation before publication_state is raised above needs_review.

defender_takeaways.detection: Search lockfiles, workflow definitions, package caches, and runner telemetry for the incident-specific selectors in this folder.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Prioritize CI runners and developer machines that resolved affected package versions or action tags during the reported window.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rebuild from clean dependencies, rotate reachable GitHub/npm/cloud credentials, and audit follow-on repository or package publishing activity.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Pin GitHub Actions to immutable SHAs, enforce package cooldowns, and alert on unexpected Bun downloads, Runner.Worker memory reads, and package install hooks.

containment_complete: Block affected packages/actions and known exfiltration destinations.
Isolate matching runners or developer hosts.
eradication_complete: Remove malicious versions/tags from internal mirrors and caches.
Rebuild affected environments from trusted baselines.
recovery_complete: Rotate exposed credentials and verify no unauthorized repositories, packages, commits, workflows, or cloud sessions remain.
closure_required: Document every matched selector and disposition unknown matches as false positive, potentially exposed, or confirmed compromise.
